statement.focus_question: Let $N(k,\ell)$ be the minimal $N$ such that for any $f:\{1,\ldots,N\}\to\{-1,1\}$ there must exist a $k$-term arithmetic progression $P$ such that\[ \left\lvert \sum_{n\in P}f(n)\right\rvert\geq \ell.\]Find good upper bounds for $N(k,\ell)$. Is it true that for any $c>0$ there exists some $C>1$ such that\[N(k,ck)\leq C^k?
statement.normalized: Let $N(k,\ell)$ be the minimal $N$ such that for any $f:\{1,\ldots,N\}\to\{-1,1\}$ there must exist a $k$-term arithmetic progression $P$ such that\[ \left\lvert \sum_{n\in P}f(n)\right\rvert\geq \ell.\]Find good upper bounds for $N(k,\ell)$. Is it true that for any $c>0$ there exists some $C>1$ such that\[N(k,ck)\leq C^k?\]What about\[N(k,2)\leq C^k\]or\[N(k,\sqrt{k})\leq C^k?\]
statement.original: Let $N(k,\ell)$ be the minimal $N$ such that for any $f:\{1,\ldots,N\}\to\{-1,1\}$ there must exist a $k$-term arithmetic progression $P$ such that\[ \left\lvert \sum_{n\in P}f(n)\right\rvert\geq \ell.\]Find good upper bounds for $N(k,\ell)$. Is it true that for any $c>0$ there exists some $C>1$ such that\[N(k,ck)\leq C^k?\]What about\[N(k,2)\leq C^k\]or\[N(k,\sqrt{k})\leq C^k?\]
